Indicative Term Sheet — Proposed acquisition of HelixPort Analytics S.A. by OrbiNova SE (EU issuer on the Mercurion Exchange EuroCap MTF, supervised by the Lydovia Securities and Markets Commission).
Structure: OrbiNova to acquire 100% via cash and share exchange; consideration €128m cash plus 4.5m new Class B ordinary shares (no security identifier assigned before admission); completion subject to OrbiNova and HelixPort shareholder approvals and Competent Authority clearance.
This document contains precise non-public information regarding OrbiNova and HelixPort that is likely to significantly affect OrbiNova’s price under MAR; wall-crossing confirmations are required and insider lists will be maintained until a public announcement.
Key non-public items include a preliminary Q3 revenue shortfall of approximately 8% versus guidance and unreleased “Aurora Mesh” pilot results; disclosure timing to be coordinated with the market operator and the LSMC.
GDPR/Art 9 schedule: explicit consent and legal basis will cover biometric access control and timekeeping (fingerprint templates and iris-scan geometry) and occupational health files for Ms. Selene Ardent (plant supervisor), who is a member of the Lydovia Techworkers Union and receives treatment for a managed cardiac condition.
Additional sensitive fields: Ms. Ardent’s prior genetic carrier screening undertaken for safety clearance and her declared political party membership (Green Horizon Lydovia) are to be pseudonymised with least-privilege access; her dependent child Leo Ardent (age 16) is listed for emergency contacts only.
Cross-border transfers: personal data, including special-category data, will remain within the EU unless strictly necessary for support by OrbiNova’s Azuristan centre under SCCs; a Transfer Impact Assessment, encryption, and audit logging are conditions precedent.
CSRD/SFDR: post-closing, OrbiNova will consolidate ESRS metrics, including workforce diversity and unionisation rates, and align product-level sustainability data to SFDR PAI indicators; generic DEI wording (inclusive culture, equal opportunity) is aspirational and non-binding.
Governance and MAR workflow: PDMR dealing blackouts apply; insider lists must include identity, role, and contact details with time stamps and reasons; Mr. Corin Feldar (CFO) will administer lists and wall-crossing notices.
Financing: €90m term loan and €50m sustainability-linked revolving credit facility tied to CSRD targets; funds earmarked for integration of the “Polaris Cloud” product suite and working capital.
Conditions precedent: audited financials, merger control clearances in Lydovia and Norvia, Works Council information/consultation, vendor warranties, and confirmation from the LSMC on lawful deferral of disclosure under MAR.
Data minimisation and security: references to religion, political opinions, health, and biometric data are limited to HR/compliance purposes with strict retention; the health of the balance sheet will be assessed by lenders in parallel.
Miscellaneous: references to Novagrad and Velstadt, internal nicknames like the Bluehammer Policy, and mentions of the Velstadt Lions sports team are informal and carry no legal effect on the transaction.
Contacts: Dr. Éloi Tarsen (General Counsel) and Sir Alar Vexholm (Non-Executive Chair), OrbiNova SE, Novagrad; communications via dealteam@orbinova.eu.
